nodes.name: 唯一性证明
nodes.definition: A uniqueness proof shows that if an object satisfying some property exists, it is the only one. The standard technique is to assume two objects a and b both satisfy the property and then prove a = b.

nodes.name: UniversalQuantifier导论
nodes.definition: The universal quantifier ∀x denotes 'for all x in the domain'. A universal statement ∀x P(x) is true if and only if P(x) is true for every element x in the domain. Most mathematical theorems are unive

nodes.name: VacuousTruth与TrivialCases
nodes.definition: A conditional P → Q is vacuously true when the hypothesis P is false (the conclusion Q is never reached). A proof is trivial when it follows immediately from definitions or previous results without ne

nodes.name: Weak归纳
nodes.definition: Weak induction (or standard induction) proves a statement P(n) for all natural numbers by: (1) proving the base case P(1) or P(0), and (2) proving that if P(k) is true, then P(k+1) is true. The induct

nodes.name: 势阱Ordering原理
nodes.definition: The well-ordering principle states every non-empty set of positive integers has a least element. Logically equivalent to mathematical induction, it can prove statements by assuming a counterexample ex

nodes.name: 3D坐标系统
nodes.definition: The 3D Cartesian coordinate system extends 2D coordinates by adding a third perpendicular axis (z), allowing us to locate points in three-dimensional space using ordered triples (x, y, z). This system

nodes.name: 应用Double积分
nodes.definition: Double integrals compute area (∬_R 1 dA), volume under a surface (∬_R f dA for f ≥ 0), and mass of a lamina with density ρ (∬_R ρ(x,y) dA). These applications anchor multivariable integration to geome

nodes.name: 应用积分面积质量
nodes.definition: Double integrals compute: area as ∬_D dA, mass as ∬_D ρ(x, y) dA (with density ρ), moments M_x = ∬_D y ρ(x, y) dA and M_y = ∬_D x ρ(x, y) dA, and center of mass (x̄, ȳ) = (M_y/M, M_x/M).

nodes.name: 应用Multivariable
nodes.definition: Multivariable calculus models physical phenomena: optimization (minimal surfaces, maximum profit), work and energy (line integrals), fluid flow and electromagnetism (divergence theorem, Stokes' theore

nodes.name: 应用Triple积分
nodes.definition: Triple integrals compute volume of solids, mass with density functions, and center of mass. The choice of coordinates (Cartesian, cylindrical, spherical) depends on the region's symmetry, dramatically

nodes.name: 弧长度曲线3D
nodes.definition: For a parametric curve r(t) from t=a to t=b, the arc length is L = ∫[a,b] |r'(t)| dt. This integrates the speed along the path. Arc length is independent of the parametrization chosen.

nodes.name: 面积体积积分
nodes.definition: Area of region R: A = ∬_R 1 dA. Volume under z = f(x,y): V = ∬_R f(x,y) dA. Double integrals generalize single-variable formulas for area and volume.

nodes.name: 链式规则Multivariable函数
nodes.definition: For z = f(x, y) where x = x(t) and y = y(t), the chain rule gives dz/dt = (∂f/∂x)(dx/dt) + (∂f/∂y)(dy/dt). For z = f(x, y) where x = x(s, t), the general case involves partial derivatives: ∂z/∂s and ∂

nodes.name: 链式规则Multivariable
nodes.definition: If f(x, y) has continuous partials and x = x(t), y = y(t), then df/dt = (∂f/∂x)(dx/dt) + (∂f/∂y)(dy/dt). For compositions like f(g(x, y), h(x, y)), the chain rule tracks how changes propagate through

nodes.name: 变化变量雅可比
nodes.definition: For transformation (u, v) = T(x, y), the Jacobian J = ∂(x, y)/∂(u, v) = det([∂x/∂u, ∂x/∂v; ∂y/∂u, ∂y/∂v]) scales area. Thus ∬_D f(x, y) dA = ∬_S f(x(u, v), y(u, v)) |J| du dv. Cylindrical and spherica

nodes.name: 变化变量Multivariable
nodes.definition: To change variables in integrals: ∬_R f(x,y) dx dy = ∬_S f(x(u,v), y(u,v)) |det(J)| du dv, where J is the Jacobian matrix. The determinant's absolute value scales area/volume.

nodes.name: Clairaut定理
nodes.definition: If ∂²f/∂x∂y and ∂²f/∂y∂x are continuous at a point, then ∂²f/∂x∂y = ∂²f/∂y∂x. This 'equality of mixed partials' shows that (for most practical functions) the order of differentiation does not matter.

nodes.name: Conservative域势
nodes.definition: A vector field F is conservative if F = ∇f for some potential function f. Conservative fields have zero curl (∇ × F = 0 for continuous partials) and satisfy the property that ∮_C F · dr = 0 for any cl

nodes.name: Conservative域
nodes.definition: A vector field F is conservative if ∫_C F · dr is independent of the path from start to end — it depends only on the endpoints. F is conservative if and only if F = ∇f for some scalar potential functi

nodes.name: Conservative载体域势
nodes.definition: A vector field F is conservative if F = ∇f for some scalar potential f. For conservative fields, ∫_C F · dr depends only on endpoints (path-independent). If F is conservative and curl-free (∂Q/∂x = ∂P

nodes.name: Conservative载体域
nodes.definition: A vector field F is conservative if F = ∇f for some potential f. Line integrals are path-independent: ∫_C F · dr = f(endpoint) - f(startpoint). In 2D, F = (P, Q) is conservative iff P_y = Q_x.

nodes.name: Constrained优化拉格朗日
nodes.definition: To optimize f(x, y) subject to constraint g(x, y) = 0, solve ∇f = λ∇g at extrema. The multiplier λ indicates the rate of change of the optimum with respect to the constraint. Check boundary and critic

nodes.name: Constrained优化
nodes.definition: Constrained optimization models engineering problems: maximizing profit subject to resource constraints, minimizing surface area for fixed volume, finding shortest paths on surfaces. Lagrange multipli

nodes.name: 连续性Multivariable
nodes.definition: Function f(x, y) is continuous at (a, b) if lim_{(x,y)→(a,b)} f(x, y) = f(a, b). In multiple variables, the limit must be the same approaching from all directions, making the concept richer than in on

nodes.name: ContourMapsLevel曲线
nodes.definition: A level curve of f(x, y) is a curve where f(x, y) = c for constant c. Contour maps show families of level curves, like topographic maps where each contour represents constant elevation. Level curves a

nodes.name: Contour Maps
nodes.definition: A contour map shows level curves of f(x, y) at equally spaced values on the xy-plane. Spacing between contours indicates steepness: close contours mean the function changes rapidly, while distant cont

nodes.name: 临界Points极值Saddle
nodes.definition: Critical points occur where ∇f = 0 (or partials fail to exist). A point is a local maximum or minimum (extremum) if f is larger or smaller than nearby values; it is a saddle point if it is larger in s

nodes.name: 临界Points极值
nodes.definition: A critical point satisfies f_x = 0 and f_y = 0 (where partials exist). Extrema occur at critical points, boundary points, or points where partials don't exist. Not all critical points are extrema; som

nodes.name: 临界PointsMultivariable分类
nodes.definition: A critical point (a, b) of f(x, y) satisfies ∇f(a, b) = 0 (or ∇f is undefined). Critical points are candidates for local maxima, local minima, or saddle points. Every continuous function on a closed b

nodes.name: 临界PointsMultivariable
nodes.definition: A critical point of f(x, y) is a point where ∇f = 0 (both partial derivatives are zero) or where ∇f is undefined. Critical points are candidates for local maxima, local minima, and saddle points. Unli

nodes.name: 杂交产物几何3D
nodes.definition: The cross product a × b produces a vector perpendicular to both a and b, with magnitude |a × b| = |a||b|sin(θ) equal to the area of the parallelogram they span. Right-hand rule determines direction: c

nodes.name: Curl与散度算子
nodes.definition: For F = ⟨P, Q, R⟩, the curl is ∇ × F = ⟨∂R/∂y − ∂Q/∂z, ∂P/∂z − ∂R/∂x, ∂Q/∂x − ∂P/∂y⟩, measuring rotation. The divergence ∇ · F = ∂P/∂x + ∂Q/∂y + ∂R/∂z measures net outflow. For conservative F, curl(F)

nodes.name: Curl与散度
nodes.definition: The curl ∇ × F measures rotation and circulation of F; for F = ⟨P, Q, R⟩, curl F = ⟨(∂R/∂y − ∂Q/∂z), (∂P/∂z − ∂R/∂x), (∂Q/∂x − ∂P/∂y)⟩. The divergence ∇ · F = ∂P/∂x + ∂Q/∂y + ∂R/∂z measures net outflo

nodes.name: Curl散度
nodes.definition: For F = (P, Q, R), curl is ∇×F = (R_y - Q_z, P_z - R_x, Q_x - P_y) (rotation), and divergence is ∇·F = P_x + Q_y + R_z (outflow). Conservative fields have curl = 0.

nodes.name: 曲率与Torsion
nodes.definition: Curvature κ = |dT/ds| measures how sharply a curve bends; torsion τ measures how much it twists out of its oscillating plane. For a curve r(t), κ = |r'(t) × r''(t)| / |r'(t)|³. Torsion reveals how muc

nodes.name: 曲率
nodes.definition: Curvature κ measures how rapidly a curve turns at each point; it is defined as κ = |dT/ds| where T = r′/|r′| is the unit tangent vector and s is arc length. In practice κ = |r′ × r″| / |r′|³. The unit

nodes.name: Cylindrical坐标
nodes.definition: Cylindrical coordinates (r, θ, z) extend polar coordinates to ℝ³ by appending a vertical z-axis. The conversion is x = r cosθ, y = r sinθ, z = z, with r² = x² + y². Cylindrical coordinates are natural

nodes.name: Differentiability Multivariable
nodes.definition: A function f(x, y) is differentiable at (a, b) if it is well-approximated by its tangent plane, with error going to zero faster than distance. Differentiability implies continuity but not vice versa.

nodes.name: Differentiability多元
nodes.definition: A function f(x, y) is differentiable at (a, b) if it can be well-approximated by a linear function; equivalently, if the error in the linear approximation vanishes faster than the distance to (a, b).

nodes.name: Directional导数梯度
nodes.definition: The directional derivative D_u f = ∇f · u gives the rate of change in direction u (unit vector). The gradient ∇f = ⟨f_x, f_y⟩ points in the direction of steepest ascent and has magnitude equal to the

nodes.name: Directional导数
nodes.definition: The directional derivative D_u f gives the rate of change of f in an arbitrary direction specified by a unit vector u. It is computed as D_u f = ∇f · u — the dot product of the gradient with the unit

nodes.name: Distance与Distance公式3D
nodes.definition: The distance between two points in 3D space (x₁, y₁, z₁) and (x₂, y₂, z₂) is √[(x₂−x₁)² + (y₂−y₁)² + (z₂−z₁)²]. This formula extends the 2D distance formula by including the z-component and defines th

nodes.name: 散度定理应用
nodes.definition: The divergence theorem states ∬_S F · n dS = ∭_W ∇ · F dV, where S is the closed surface bounding W with outward normal n. Total flux out of region W equals the integral of divergence throughout W. Us

nodes.name: 散度定理
nodes.definition: Divergence theorem: ∬_S F · dS = ∭_W (∇·F) dV, where S is the closed surface bounding W (outward normal). This relates flux through a closed surface to divergence integrated over the volume.

nodes.name: Dot杂交Products几何
nodes.definition: The dot product u·v = u₁v₁ + u₂v₂ + u₃v₃ measures alignment (equals |u||v|cos(θ)); it is zero when vectors are orthogonal. The cross product u × v produces a vector perpendicular to both, with magnitu

nodes.name: Dot产物几何
nodes.definition: The dot product a·b = |a||b|cos(θ) measures how aligned two vectors are. Geometrically, it computes the projection of one vector onto another: proj_a(b) = (a·b/|a|²)a. When a·b = 0, vectors are orthog

nodes.name: Double积分Cartesian坐标
nodes.definition: For a rectangle R = [a, b] × [c, d], the double integral ∬_R f(x, y) dA equals the iterated integral ∫_a^b ∫_c^d f(x, y) dy dx. By Fubini's theorem, the order of integration can be switched if f is co

nodes.name: Double积分Cartesian
nodes.definition: The double integral ∬_R f(x,y) dA gives the volume under z = f(x,y) above region R. For non-rectangular regions, bounds depend on neighboring variables: ∫_a^b ∫_{g₁(x)}^{g₂(x)} f(x,y) dy dx.

nodes.name: Double积分一般Regions
nodes.definition: For a region D described as {(x, y) : a ≤ x ≤ b, g₁(x) ≤ y ≤ g₂(x)}, the double integral ∬_D f(x, y) dA = ∫_a^b ∫_{g₁(x)}^{g₂(x)} f(x, y) dy dx. Describing regions correctly (both as Type I and Type I

nodes.name: Double积分极坐标坐标
nodes.definition: In polar coordinates (r, θ), the element dA = r dr dθ (not dr dθ). For a region in polar form, ∬_D f(x, y) dA = ∫_α^β ∫_{r₁(θ)}^{r₂(θ)} f(r cos θ, r sin θ) r dr dθ. Polar coordinates simplify integral

nodes.name: Double积分极坐标
nodes.definition: In polar coordinates (r, θ), the area element becomes dA = r dr dθ. The double integral becomes ∫_α^β ∫_{r₁(θ)}^{r₂(θ)} f(r cos θ, r sin θ) r dr dθ. Polar is natural for circular or radial regions.

nodes.name: Double积分RectangularRegions
nodes.definition: For a rectangular region R = [a, b] × [c, d], the double integral ∬_R f dA = ∫_a^b ∫_c^d f(x, y) dy dx is straightforward: the bounds are constants. This is the entry point for computing double integr

nodes.name: Double积分
nodes.definition: The double integral ∬_R f(x, y) dA extends integration to 2D: partition region R into small rectangles, form Riemann sums by approximating f as constant on each, and take the limit as partition size s

nodes.name: 方程LinesPlanes3D
nodes.definition: A line in 3D can be written parametrically as r(t) = r₀ + tv, where r₀ is a point on the line and v is the direction vector. A plane through point (x₀, y₀, z₀) with normal vector n = ⟨a, b, c⟩ has equ

nodes.name: 方程LinesPlanes
nodes.definition: A line in 3D is parametrized as r(t) = r₀ + td (position plus scalar multiple of direction vector). A plane with normal vector n = ⟨a, b, c⟩ passing through (x₀, y₀, z₀) has equation a(x−x₀) + b(y−y₀)

nodes.name: 通量积分
nodes.definition: The flux of F through surface S: ∬_S F · dS = ∬_S F · n dS, where n is the unit normal. If S is parametrized, ∬_S F · dS = ∬_D F · (r_u × r_v) du dv. Flux measures flow rate through the surface.

nodes.name: 函数Several变量
nodes.definition: A function of n variables is a rule f: D ⊆ ℝⁿ → ℝ mapping n-tuples (x₁, ..., xₙ) to real numbers. The domain D is the set of valid inputs; understanding domain restrictions (division by zero, logarith

nodes.name: Fundamental定理Line积分
nodes.definition: If F = ∇f (F is conservative), then ∫_C F · dr = f(B) − f(A), depending only on endpoints A and B, not the path C. This extends the single-variable fundamental theorem of calculus and provides a short

nodes.name: 梯度载体
nodes.definition: The gradient of f is the vector ∇f = ⟨∂f/∂x, ∂f/∂y⟩ (in ℝ²) or ⟨∂f/∂x, ∂f/∂y, ∂f/∂z⟩ (in ℝ³) that collects all partial derivatives. The gradient points in the direction of steepest increase of f and i

nodes.name: Greens定理应用
nodes.definition: Green's theorem relates a line integral around a closed curve C to a double integral over the enclosed region D: ∮_C P dx + Q dy = ∬_D (∂Q/∂x − ∂P/∂y) dA. It connects circulation of F to its 2D curl,

nodes.name: Greens定理
nodes.definition: Green's theorem: ∮_C (P dx + Q dy) = ∬_D (Q_x - P_y) dA. This relates line integrals around a closed curve to a double integral of curl over the region, converting circulation to an area integral.

nodes.name: 海森矩阵第二导数检验
nodes.definition: The Hessian matrix H = [[f_xx, f_xy], [f_yx, f_yy]] contains all second partial derivatives. At a critical point, the determinant det(H) and trace tr(H) determine whether it is a local max (det > 0, f

nodes.name: 高阶阶偏导数
nodes.definition: Higher-order partial derivatives are obtained by differentiating partial derivatives with respect to any variable. The second-order mixed partial ∂²f/∂x∂y means 'differentiate first with respect to y,

nodes.name: 高阶阶PartialsMixed
nodes.definition: Mixed partial derivatives ∂²f/∂x∂y and ∂²f/∂y∂x measure rate of change in two different variables. Clairaut's theorem states that if mixed partials are continuous, then ∂²f/∂x∂y = ∂²f/∂y∂x (they commu

nodes.name: 高阶阶Partials
nodes.definition: Higher-order partials are partial derivatives of partial derivatives: ∂²f/∂x², ∂²f/∂y², and mixed partials ∂²f/∂x∂y. Notation ∂²f/∂x∂y means first differentiate with respect to y, then x.

nodes.name: Implicit分化Multivariable
nodes.definition: For an implicit equation F(x, y) = 0, we can find dy/dx by differentiating with respect to x: (∂F/∂x) + (∂F/∂y)(dy/dx) = 0, so dy/dx = −(∂F/∂x)/(∂F/∂y). This extends to multiple variables and constrai

nodes.name: Iterated积分Fubini
nodes.definition: Fubini's theorem states that for continuous f on a rectangular region, ∬_R f(x, y) dA = ∫∫ f(x, y) dy dx (inner integral with respect to y, outer with respect to x). The two orders of integration give

nodes.name: Iterated积分
nodes.definition: A double integral ∬ f(x,y) dA can be computed as an iterated integral ∫∫ f(x,y) dy dx. By Fubini's theorem, the order can be swapped if f is continuous; rewriting bounds requires understanding the reg

nodes.name: 雅可比变化变量
nodes.definition: When changing variables in a double integral using the substitution x = g(u, v), y = h(u, v), the area element transforms as dA = |J| du dv, where J is the Jacobian determinant J = ∂(x,y)/∂(u,v) = det

nodes.name: 拉格朗日Multipliers
nodes.definition: To optimize f(x, y) subject to g(x, y) = 0, solve ∇f = λ∇g along with the constraint. The Lagrange multiplier λ represents the sensitivity of the optimum to relaxing the constraint.

nodes.name: Level集合曲面
nodes.definition: A level set is the set of all points where f(x, y, z) = k for a fixed constant k. For a function f: ℝ² → ℝ, level sets are curves; for f: ℝ³ → ℝ, level sets are surfaces. These visualize how a scalar

nodes.name: 极限连续性Multivariable
nodes.definition: For a multivariable function, lim_(x,y)→(a,b) f(x,y) = L if for every ε > 0 there exists δ > 0 such that |f(x,y) − L| < ε whenever 0 < √[(x−a)² + (y−b)²] < δ. Continuity requires the limit to exist an

nodes.name: Line积分标量
nodes.definition: The line integral ∫_C f ds integrates a scalar function along a curve, weighted by arc length. Parametrically: ∫_C f ds = ∫_a^b f(r(t)) ||r'(t)|| dt. This generalizes single-variable integration to cu

nodes.name: Line积分载体域
nodes.definition: The line integral ∫_C F · dr integrates a vector field along a curve. Parametrically: ∫_C F · dr = ∫_a^b F(r(t)) · r'(t) dt. This represents work done by force F along the path.

nodes.name: Line积分
nodes.definition: Line integrals ∫_C f ds (scalar) or ∫_C F · dr (vector) integrate along curves. The scalar version sums f weighted by arc length; the vector version computes work done by F along a path C. Both depend

nodes.name: Mixed Partials Clairaut
nodes.definition: Mixed partial derivatives are equal when they are continuous: ∂²f/∂x∂y = ∂²f/∂y∂x, by Clairaut's theorem. This equality simplifies computation and reflects symmetry in the function's behavior.

nodes.name: Multivariable函数入门Domain
nodes.definition: A function f: D → ℝ maps points (x, y) or (x, y, z) in domain D to real numbers. The domain is the set of inputs where f is defined; the range is the set of outputs. Domains in ℝ² are regions (open, c

nodes.name: Multivariable函数入门
nodes.definition: A multivariable function f: ℝⁿ → ℝ takes multiple inputs (x, y, z, …) and produces one output. The graph lives in ℝⁿ⁺¹. Level sets (contours) visualize f by showing where f takes constant values, like

nodes.name: Multivariable极限
nodes.definition: The limit lim_{(x,y)→(a,b)} f(x, y) = L means f(x, y) approaches L as (x, y) approaches (a, b) along every possible path. This is fundamentally harder than single-variable limits: one-variable limits

nodes.name: 优化Multivariable基础
nodes.definition: To optimize f(x, y) on a region: find critical points, classify them, evaluate f at critical points and on the boundary, then compare. Global extrema on closed bounded sets are guaranteed to exist.

nodes.name: Parametric曲面
nodes.definition: A parametric surface is described by a vector function r(u, v) = ⟨x(u,v), y(u,v), z(u,v)⟩ mapping a 2D parameter domain to a surface in ℝ³. The partial derivatives r_u and r_v are tangent vectors to t

nodes.name: 偏导数
nodes.definition: The partial derivative ∂f/∂x is the ordinary derivative with respect to x while holding all other variables constant. Partial derivatives measure instantaneous rates of change along coordinate axes an

nodes.name: 速率变化偏导数
nodes.definition: ∂f/∂x represents how fast f increases per unit change in x when y is held fixed. Geometrically, it is the slope of the curve obtained by intersecting the surface z = f(x,y) with a plane of constant y.

nodes.name: 第二Partials检验极值
nodes.definition: At critical point (a, b), compute the Hessian matrix H = [[f_xx, f_xy], [f_xy, f_yy]]. If det(H) > 0 and f_xx > 0, it's a local min; if f_xx < 0, local max. If det(H) < 0, it's a saddle point. If det(

nodes.name: 第二Partials检验海森
nodes.definition: The Hessian H = [[f_xx, f_xy], [f_xy, f_yy]] classifies critical points. If det(H) > 0 and f_xx > 0, it's a local minimum. If det(H) > 0 and f_xx < 0, it's a maximum. If det(H) < 0, it's a saddle poin

nodes.name: 第二Partials检验
nodes.definition: At a critical point (a, b) of f(x, y), the discriminant D = f_xx f_yy − (f_xy)² classifies the critical point. If D > 0 and f_xx > 0, it is a local minimum; if D > 0 and f_xx < 0, it is a local maximu

nodes.name: 空间曲线
nodes.definition: A space curve r(t) has tangent vector r'(t), which indicates direction of motion. Arc length parameterization uses s(t) as the parameter and gives unit tangent T(s) = dr/ds. Curvature κ measures how q

nodes.name: 球面坐标
nodes.definition: Spherical coordinates (ρ, φ, θ) describe a point by its distance ρ from the origin, polar angle φ from the positive z-axis (0 ≤ φ ≤ π), and azimuthal angle θ in the xy-plane (0 ≤ θ < 2π). The conversi

nodes.name: Stokes与散度Theorems
nodes.definition: Stokes' theorem: ∮_C F · dr = ∬_S (∇ × F) · dS (line integral of F around a closed curve equals flux of curl through the surface). Divergence theorem: ∬_S F · dS = ∭_V ∇ · F dV (flux through a closed

nodes.name: Stokes定理应用
nodes.definition: Stokes' theorem states ∮_C F · dr = ∬_S (∇ × F) · n dS, where S is a surface bounded by closed curve C. It generalizes Green's theorem to 3D: circulation around C equals flux of curl through S. Bounda

nodes.name: Stokes定理
nodes.definition: Stokes' theorem: ∮_C F · dr = ∬_S (∇×F) · dS. This relates circulation around a closed curve to the flux of curl through the surface, generalizing Green's theorem to 3D.

nodes.name: 表面面积积分
nodes.definition: The surface area of a parametrized surface r(u, v) is A = ∬_D |r_u × r_v| du dv. For a scalar integral over surface S, ∬_S f dS = ∬_D f(r(u, v)) |r_u × r_v| du dv. This extends to vector integrals via

nodes.name: 表面积分通量载体
nodes.definition: The flux of F through oriented surface S is ∬_S F · n dS = ∬_S F · (r_u × r_v) du dv, measuring flow through the surface. When F is the curl of another vector field, Stokes' theorem relates this to a

nodes.name: 表面积分通量
nodes.definition: The surface integral ∬_S F · dS computes flux (net flow of F through S). Using parametrization r(u, v), dS = (r_u × r_v) du dv, and the integral becomes ∬_D F(r(u,v)) · (r_u × r_v) du dv. Orientation

nodes.name: 表面积分标量函数
nodes.definition: ∬_S f(x, y, z) dS integrates a scalar function over a surface. For parametrization r(u, v), this equals ∬_D f(r(u, v)) |r_u × r_v| du dv. Applications include mass of a thin shell (integrating density

nodes.name: 表面积分标量
nodes.definition: The surface integral ∬_S f dS integrates f over surface S. If S is parametrized as r(u,v), then dS = ||r_u × r_v|| du dv, and ∬_S f dS = ∬_D f(r(u,v)) ||r_u × r_v|| du dv.

nodes.name: 表面ParametrizationTangent

nodes.definition: A space is normal if disjoint closed sets F, G have disjoint open neighborhoods. Every compact Hausdorff space and every metric space is normal.

nodes.definition: A space is regular if for closed F and x ∉ F, there exist disjoint open sets separating them. Regularity separates points from closed sets. Every metric space is regular.

nodes.definition: In a normal space, every continuous function f: A → ℝ from a closed subset A extends to a continuous function F: X → ℝ.

nodes.name: 拓扑流形导论
nodes.definition: A topological manifold is a Hausdorff space with a countable basis where every point has a neighborhood homeomorphic to an open ball in ℝⁿ. Manifolds are the natural spaces for calculus and geometry.

nodes.name: Tychonoffs定理
nodes.definition: An arbitrary product of compact spaces is compact in the product topology. This deep result is equivalent to the Axiom of Choice. Compactness is stable under infinite products.
